source.title: Non-Condensable Gas System Explosion at PCA DeRidder Paper Mill

actions_taken: OSHA citations issued for emergency response plan violations
event.summary: On February 8, 2017, at approximately 11:05 am CDT, a foul condensate tank at the PCA DeRidder containerboard mill exploded during annual maintenance outage. The tank, part of a non-condensable gas system, contained water with a layer of flammable turpentine and an explosive vapor space. Air entered the tank through a pressure vacuum breaker during normal pressure swings and cooling, mixing with turpentine vapor to create an explosive atmosphere. Hot work (welding) on water piping above the tank likely ignited the explosive mixture. The explosion killed three contractors and injured seven others, launching the 100,000-gallon tank approximately 375 feet over a six-story building. The incident resulted from inadequate process hazard analysis, ineffective safeguards, and failure to apply process safety management systems to the non-condensable gas system.
recommendations: Evaluate and replace foul condensate tank with vessel designed for full-vacuum conditions
Install explosion prevention systems on non-condensable gas equipment
Design foul condensate tank with weak roof-to-shell attachment (frangible roof)
Implement robust administrative controls for non-condensable gas system operations
Conduct process hazard analysis for non-condensable gas system
Expand process safety management program boundaries beyond regulatory requirements
Apply hierarchy of controls to non-condensable gas system safeguards
Evaluate inherently safer design options to eliminate air ingress pathways
Establish clear ownership and responsibility for foul condensate tank operations
Apply TAPPI TIP 0416-09, NFPA 69, NFPA 68, and ISA 84 standards to non-condensable gas systems
Reiterate OSHA Process Safety Management standard coverage to include atmospheric storage tanks

hazards.name: Oxygen ingress into non-condensable gas system
Hot work in proximity to flammable vapor sources

threats.name: Air entry through pressure vacuum breaker during pressure swings
Air entry during tank cooling in shutdown conditions
Mixing of air with turpentine vapor above upper explosive limit
Welding hot work as ignition source near explosive atmosphere
Inadequate combustible gas monitoring for hot work permit

consequences.name: Vapor space explosion in foul condensate tank
Fatalities from explosion blast and thermal effects
Injuries from explosion blast, thermal effects, and projectiles
Structural damage to tank and surrounding equipment
Damage to pipe rack and associated piping systems

controls.name: Pressure vacuum breaker on foul condensate tank
controls.lod_basis: Designed to relieve low-pressure conditions by pulling air into tank

supporting_text: Air likely entered the foul condensate tank through a vacuum relief device on the tank's roof

During typical operation, automatic controls continually cycled the liquid level inside the tank, creating routine periods of low pressure. These low-pressure conditions were relieved by the vacuum relief device, which pulled air into the tank
During the annual outage, air also likely entered the tank as its contents cooled. This cooling created another low-pressure condition within the tank

controls.name: Rupture disks on CNCG piping system
controls.barrier_role: Prevent explosion overpressure damage to piping
controls.lod_basis: Designed to protect CNCG system piping from overpressure scenarios

supporting_text: Rupture disks are also located in the CNCG piping systems to prevent explosion overpressure damage

The rupture disks used on the CNCG system were designed to protect the CNCG system piping from overpressure scenarios. The rupture disks were not designed to protect units such as the foul condensate tank from overpressure scenarios

controls.name: Flame arrestors in CNCG system
controls.lod_basis: Designed to halt flame propagation and protect equipment

supporting_text: CNCG system piping is typically equipped with flame arrestors near the gas source, such as the foul condensate tank, and at the gas discharge, such as the boilers used at the PCA DeRidder mill to destroy the NCG
Flame arrestors in the CNCG header system are designed to prevent fire in this piping from spreading to other connected units

controls.name: Hot work permit system
controls.barrier_role: Control ignition sources during hot work activities
controls.lod_basis: Routine procedure to assess hazards and issue permits for hot work

supporting_text: On the morning of February 8, a PCA DeRidder safety coordinator initiated a hot work permit for the water piping welding repairs, a routine procedure for what appeared to be a typical outage work task

Another PCA employee used a gas detector to check for the presence of flammables around and inside the water piping. With no indications of a flammable atmosphere in the water piping or the platform around the hot work area, PCA issued the hot work permit for the work at approximately 8:00 am

The permit focused on the water piping, pipe bridge, and surrounding platform, however, the permit did not consider the potential hazards within adjacent vessels or piping, such as inside the foul condensate tank
The PCA employees involved in issuing the hot work permit were familiar with the operation of the process, but they did not know that the vapor inside the tank was explosive

controls.name: Combustible gas monitoring for hot work
controls.barrier_role: Detect flammable atmospheres before hot work
controls.lod_basis: Gas detector used to check for flammables around hot work area

supporting_text: Another PCA employee used a gas detector to check for the presence of flammables around and inside the water piping. With no indications of a flammable atmosphere in the water piping or the platform around the hot work area, PCA issued the hot work permit
The permit did not consider the potential hazards within adjacent vessels or piping, such as inside the foul condensate tank

controls.name: Valve closure and isolation of water piping
controls.barrier_role: Isolate water piping from foul condensate tank
controls.lod_basis: Closed and locked valve to separate repair area from tank

supporting_text: Workers consistently informed the CSB that before starting welding operations, PCA closed and locked the valve on the foul condensate tank side of the water piping. The valve was never located after the explosion
On February 7, 2017, PCA operations personnel isolated the water piping by closing and locking valves in the system

controls.name: Process hazard analysis for non-condensable gas system
controls.lod_basis: Systematic evaluation of hazards to inform control selection

supporting_text: The DeRidder mill never conducted a process hazard analysis to identify, evaluate, and control process hazards for the non-condensable gas system

Evaluate the majority of the non-condensable gas system, including the foul condensate tank, for certain hazards. The DeRidder mill never conducted a process hazard analysis to identify, evaluate, and control process hazards for the non-condensable gas system

controls.name: Process safety management system coverage
controls.barrier_role: Apply PSM requirements to non-condensable gas system
controls.lod_basis: Regulatory and voluntary application of PSM to manage process hazards

supporting_text: Although not required by Federal regulation, good-practice guidance recommends developing and implementing a robust safety management system to manage the hazards related to generating, collecting, and treating of non-condensable gases. PCA did not voluntarily apply its process safety management system to the non-condensable gas system
Expand the boundaries of its process safety management program beyond the units covered by safety regulations

controls.name: Hierarchy of controls application
controls.lod_basis: Systematic approach to control selection prioritizing elimination and substitution

supporting_text: Effectively apply the hierarchy of controls to the selection and implementation of safeguards that the company used to prevent a potential non-condensable gas explosion
Application of the Hierarchy of Controls at the PCA DeRidder Mill

controls.name: Inherently safer design evaluation
controls.lod_basis: Replace foul condensate tank with full-vacuum capable vessel or install explosion prevention systems

supporting_text: Evaluate inherently safer design options that could have eliminated the possibility of air entering the non-condensable gas system, including the foul condensate tank
Replace the Foul Condensate Tank with A Vessel Designed for Full-Vacuum
Install Explosion Prevention Systems

controls.name: Industry safety standards application (TAPPI, NFPA, ISA)
controls.barrier_role: Apply recognized good engineering practices for NCG systems
controls.lod_basis: TAPPI TIP 0416-09, NFPA 69, NFPA 68, ISA 84 standards for NCG explosion prevention

supporting_text: Apply important aspects of industry safety guidance and standards, including the Technical Association of the Pulp and Paper Industry Technical Information Paper 0416-09, National Fire Protection Association Standard 69, National Fire Protection Association Standard 68, and International Society of Automation Standard 84
CNCG systems must be designed and operated to prevent ingress of air. No open flames or welding should be allowed on or around the vessels or process lines of a CNCG system

controls.name: Operational ownership and responsibility assignment
controls.barrier_role: Establish clear accountability for foul condensate tank operations
controls.lod_basis: Organizational clarity on equipment ownership and operational responsibility

supporting_text: Establish which mill operations group held ownership of, and responsibility for, the foul condensate tank

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
